description: Identifies volatility compression using Bollinger Band width reaching its 30-day minimum, then enters when price breaks out with a 2x ATR move from the squeeze zone, confirmed by RSI divergence. Long entries occur on bullish breakouts with bullish RSI divergence; short entries on bearish breakouts with bearish RSI divergence.

conditions: Bollinger Band width (upper - lower) is at 30-day minimum (lowest value in last 1440 bars)
Bullish RSI divergence: price made lower low over last 14 bars while RSI(14) made higher low over same period
Bollinger Band width (upper - lower) is at 30-day minimum (lowest value in last 1440 bars)
Bearish RSI divergence: price made higher high over last 14 bars while RSI(14) made lower high over same period

notes: Bollinger Band width calculation: upper_band - lower_band. 30-day minimum = 1440 bars on 15-minute timeframe. RSI divergence detection requires comparing current RSI value with RSI 14 bars ago and corresponding price levels. Ensure no future bias when identifying 'lowest width in 30 days' - use rolling minimum up to current bar only.
